LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ELEVATE INDEPENDENT SERVICES, INC., IRELLA BLACKWOOD'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AS TO PLAINTIFFS SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF BOBBY SISK'S UCL CLAIM, BY INCORPORATING BY REFERENCE EARLIER ALLEGATIONS, SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES THAT MR. SISK LOST MONEY OR PROPERTY AS A RESULT OF DEFENDANTS' DEFAMATORY STATEMENTS, WHICH ARE THEMSELVES A PREDICATE FOR THE UNLAWFUL PRONG OF THE UCL. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG; DEPT. 302.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-28-2017 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-18-2017 Continued To Oct-02-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-15-546236, CGC-16-551933 WITH CASE CGC-16-550284
Off Calendar May-15-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. CASEY S. CLOW, TEL. NO. 1-415-732-7788 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ELEVATE INDEPENDENT SERVICES, INC. and IRELLA BLACKWOOD's MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-15-546236, CGC-16-551933 WITH CASE CGC-16-550284 is GRANTED. The instant case (CGC-550284) is consolidated with case CGC-15-546236 and CGC-15-546236 is designated the lead case. The cases are consolidated for trial purposes and the parties are ordered to coordinate discovery. Consolidation is warranted because there are overlapping issues regarding whether the "Owner's Representative Agreement" is enforceable and Mr. Sisk's damages. Consolidation will lead to only a two month continuance for the trial based on the October 2, 2017 trial date in the lead case. The interests of justice weigh in favor of consolidation and the prejudice to Mr. Sisk from consolidation is minimal. Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =302/ru

Notice Of Elevate Independent Services, Inc.S And Irella Blackwoods Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Plaintiffs Sixth Cause Of Action
Off Calendar Feb-09-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Courtney M. Coates, of Law Offices of Courtney M. Coates, Tel. 951.587.3988, for plaintiffs;
Casey S. Clow, of Craigie, McCarthy & Clow, Tel. 415.732.7788, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ELEVATE INDEPENDENT SERVICES, INC., IRELLA BLACKWOOD's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Plaintiffs Sixth Cause Of Action is DENIED. Plaintiff Bobby Sisk's UCL claim, by incorporating by reference earlier allegations, sufficiently alleges that Mr. Sisk lost money or property as a result of defendants' defamatory statements, which are themselves a predicate for the unlawful prong of the UCL. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
